these numbers are achieved because most streets a lined with five or six story buildings with shops on the ground floors and accommodation above. this is the classic paris streetscape.

by comparison there are long stretches of king, queen, dundas and bloor streets and eglinton and lawrence avenues  that have buildings of only two or three stories. if slightly higher buildings replaced the present structures density would rise and livability maintained.  there are two examples on bloor just west of jane.
